verdict_reasoning: HSPD1 (HSP60) is canonically a mitochondrial matrix chaperonin, but has well-documented surface display on tumor cells and stressed cells (heat shock, oxidative stress), where it acts as a damage-associated molecular pattern and immunogenic target. Cancer ecto-HSP60 is recognized as a tumor-associated surface antigen and has been pursued as an immunotherapy target. This qualifies as cell-state-induced surfacing despite the dominant mitochondrial localization.
